context: User invokes /bruhs with no arguments inside a project that already has .claude/bruhs.json. They want to start building a new feature but forgot the exact subcommand.
expected_behavior: Presents an AskUserQuestion menu listing the bruhs subcommands (spawn, claim, cook, yeet, peep, doodle, dip) without including slop, per SKILL.md
Waits for the user's selection before performing any filesystem or git operations
After the user picks 'cook', routes to the cook workflow defined in commands/cook.md beginning at Step 0 (check .claude/bruhs.json)
Does not fabricate a feature description — either prompts the user for one or enters cook's interactive sub-flow
failure_modes: Silently picks a default subcommand instead of asking via AskUserQuestion
Includes slop in the interactive menu despite SKILL.md stating it is intentionally excluded
Jumps directly into cook's Step 1 exploration without receiving a feature description or ticket ID
